title: The funeral of Boston firefighter Robert Kilduff Jr., in photos
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Funeral held for Boston firefighter Robert T. Kilduff Jr. at the Cathedral of the Holy Cross on June 1, 2026.
Kilduff died fighting a Dorchester fire on May 23 after falling through a weakened floor while saving colleagues.
Boston Mayor Michelle Wu and Fire Commissioner Rodney Marshall were among the speakers honoring Kilduff's sacrifices.

title: A ‘fishy squisher’: NASA confirms meteorite landed in Cape Cod Bay
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A meteor released energy equivalent to 300 tons of TNT, breaking apart 40 miles above New England.
The meteorite fragments landed in Cape Cod Bay, with the largest pieces in 111 feet of water.
Over 80 witnesses reported seeing or hearing the meteor as far as Ontario, Canada.

title: Theo Epstein is reportedly disappointed by Red Sox’ ‘intense’ focus on analytics
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Theo Epstein, Fenway Sports Group adviser, reportedly disappointed by Red Sox's intense focus on analytics.
Epstein, former Red Sox GM, views Craig Breslow as a mentee, questions Breslow's heavy reliance on data models.
The Red Sox, in last place in AL East, face scrutiny as they struggle with recent losses and analytical approach.

title: Here’s what Boston meteorologists are saying about warmer temperatures this week
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Boston temperatures are expected to rise into the 80s by late week, according to local meteorologists.
A cool start to June in Boston with temperatures in the low 60s, warming later in the week.
Dry weather is anticipated into Saturday with potential showers by Sunday due to a cold front.

title: Watch: The best videos of Saturday’s meteor boom over New England
author: Emily Turner
bullets: A meteor exploded off the New England coast on May 30 at 2:11 p.m., creating a loud sonic boom.
The event was captured by doorbell cameras, dashcams, and a video featuring a blueberry muffin costume.
NASA reported the meteor fragmented 40 miles above northeast Massachusetts and southeast New Hampshire.

title: Man charged in road rage machete incident in Dedham
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Mussa M. Rafat Mohamed, 51, of Newton, allegedly targeted another vehicle with a machete in a road rage incident in Dedham.
Mohamed was arrested at his Newton home and charged with assault and battery, among other charges, in Dedham District Court.
Mohamed's medical license was previously reprimanded in 2024 for assault and restraining order violation; he's held without bail pending a hearing.

title: Campbell sues UnitedHealthcare, says it defrauded MassHealth of $100 million
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Attorney General Andrea Campbell sues UnitedHealthcare for allegedly defrauding MassHealth of over $100 million.
The lawsuit claims UnitedHealthcare overstated members' conditions to gain higher reimbursement rates from 2015 to 2025.
UnitedHealthcare denies allegations, asserting their program meets seniors' individual health needs and calling the lawsuit meritless.

title: New England’s most welcoming towns and best summer escapes
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Bar Harbor, Maine and Burlington, Vermont named among America's most welcoming towns, hosting summer festivals and events.
Bristol, R.I. marks its 241st year with the oldest continuous 4th of July celebration in the U.S.
Logan Airport's new remote terminal in Framingham allows JetBlue and Delta passengers to bypass security lines.

title: Shaquille O’Neal: Celtics should fix their play-style instead of breaking up Tatum and Brown
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Shaquille O'Neal advises the Boston Celtics to maintain their core duo of Jayson Tatum and Jaylen Brown.
O'Neal suggests the Celtics should focus on improving in-game adjustments rather than pursuing trades for players like Antetokounmpo.
Celtics' playoff strategy heavily relied on 3-point shooting, leading to their downfall against Philadelphia in the series.

title: They caught the meteor boom on camera — while dressed as a blueberry muffin
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Drag performer Andi Van Dyke's video capturing a meteor boom went viral, nearing one million views on Instagram.
The meteor caused a sonic boom that rattled windows and confused residents across Massachusetts, drawing emergency responses.
Van Dyke was filming in a muffin costume, which had been used for a prior food-themed show, when the event occurred.
